trade.summary: Trade remains modest but has been growing. Brazil exports soybeans, corn, and wheat to Indonesia. Indonesia exports palm oil, electronics, and rubber to Brazil. Both countries see potential for deeper agricultural and commodity trade.

diplomatic.summary: Both are large emerging economies with strategic autonomy policies. Brazil and Indonesia coordinate in G20 as major developing nations. Shared interest in multilateral reform and climate issues, particularly around tropical forest conservation.

military.summary: Very limited military ties. No alliance. Some defense industry contacts but no significant arms transfers or joint exercises.

financial.summary: Limited FDI in both directions. Some Brazilian agricultural companies have explored Indonesian investments. Indonesian conglomerates have minimal presence in Brazil.

energy.summary: Indonesia exports some coal to Brazil. Both are significant biofuel producers. Limited bilateral energy trade.

scientific.summary: Growing but still limited scientific cooperation. Shared interests in tropical ecology and sustainable agriculture. Some exchange programs between agricultural research institutions.
